raw_consumer_statement:
My American Airlines Aviator card issued by Barclay 's was charged {$2800.00} on XX/XX/XXXX. I did not make this charge. I reported the charge to the credit card company and received a new card. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, Barclays informed me that " documentation '' from the merchant showed the charge is legitimate and they reinstated the charge to my card. 
When I called Barclays on XX/XX/XXXX, they gave me the website where I supposedly placed the order and the IP address from which I supposedly place the order. 
When I investigated the website, I learned that it does not exist and never existed. The IP address arose from a different city from my residence or work. In addition, the products that I supposedly ordered do not exist. Of course, I never received any merchandise. 
When I informed Barclays about this information, they stated that I need to pay the charge. 
I did not make the order, the order is completely fraudulent, and I never received any merchandise. Barclays is violating their No Fraud guarantee and should not be permitted to make me pay for this.
